subject: Business Studies
chapter: BS02 — Principles of Management (Fayol's 14 Principles, Taylor's Scientific Management)

questions.question: During its 1st internal review, Aarav Textiles has assigned separate supervisors for packing, quality check and dispatch in its spinning mill. Which principle best explains why this arrangement may increase efficiency, along with the idea that management principles are broad guidelines?
questions.explanation:
Division of work means tasks are divided into specialised parts.
Specialisation raises efficiency and skill, which is why the case fits this principle.
The reference to management principles as broad guidelines links the case to the general, flexible nature of principles.

questions.question: During the 2nd review, Nisha Foods' manager follows one clear line of authority from the plant head to the shop floor before approving overtime in the retail chain. Which Fayol principle is being used together with a formal communication path?
questions.explanation:
Scalar chain means a clear line of authority from top to bottom.
It supports orderly communication and approval in the organisation.
This is different from unity of command, which is about receiving orders from only one superior.

questions.question: At the 3rd review, a supervisor at Metro Plastics believes that workers should first be selected scientifically, then trained for maximum output in the logistics warehouse. This idea is most closely connected with Taylor’s principle of
options: Development of each and every person to his or her greatest efficiency and prosperity
questions.explanation:
Taylor stressed scientific selection and training of workers.
The aim is to improve both efficiency and prosperity of the worker and the organisation.
This is a key part of scientific management, not a Fayol principle.

questions.question: At the 4th review, Kiran Logistics introduces standard tools and a fixed best way to carry out a task in its packaging unit. Which Taylor technique is being applied along with the principle of scientific management?
questions.explanation:
Standardisation means using uniform tools, methods and materials.
Simplification removes unnecessary variety and reduces waste.
It is a technique of scientific management and fits Taylor's emphasis on science rather than rule of thumb.

questions.question:
At the 41st review, which of the following statements is correct about principles of management and their significance?
I. They help in thoughtful managerial decisions.
II. They are exact mathematical laws that never change.
III. They improve coordination in an organisation.
Choose the correct option.
questions.explanation:
Principles of management help managers take better decisions and improve coordination.
They are not exact mathematical laws, so statement II is wrong.
Hence only I and III are correct.

questions.question: During the 42nd review, at Nova Packaging in the electronics assembly line, workers are allowed to suggest a better packing layout, and the manager approves it because it raises output. Which Fayol principle is highlighted together with the behavioural nature of management?
questions.explanation:
Initiative means encouraging workers to take the first step and suggest useful ideas.
The case shows worker participation, which is closely linked to the behavioural aspect of management.
Approval of the idea because it improves output fits the spirit of initiative.

questions.question: At the 43rd review, which option is NOT a technique of Taylor's scientific management in Bright Tech's electronics assembly line?
questions.explanation:
Unity of command is a Fayol principle, not a Taylor technique.
Time study, motion study and differential piece wage plan belong to scientific management.
So the odd one out is unity of command.

questions.question:
Read the statements for the 44th review.
I. In scientific management, management and workers should work in harmony.
II. In Fayol's principles, subordination of individual interest means personal goals should dominate organisational goals.
III. Division of work helps in specialisation.
Which statements are correct?
questions.explanation:
Statement I is correct because Taylor emphasised harmony, not conflict.
Statement II is incorrect because organisational interest should come before individual interest.
Statement III is correct because division of work promotes specialisation and efficiency.

questions.question: During the 45th review, Summit Prints has a fixed schedule, clear rules, and everyone knows their place in the organisation. A consultant says the firm is applying one Fayol principle and one general nature of management simultaneously. Which pair is most suitable?
options: Equity and management as a random activity
Unity of command and management as intuition only
Order and management as a systematic process
Esprit de corps and management as a rigid law
questions.explanation:
Order means the right person and material in the right place.
A fixed schedule and clear rules also show management as a systematic, goal-oriented process.
The other options mix unrelated ideas or distort the nature of management.

questions.question: Case 101st: Sanjay, the manager of Dawn Furnishings, noticed that in the storekeeping section of the electronics assembly line, the same worker was answering two different supervisors. This created confusion, conflicting instructions and delays. Which combination best explains the issue and the correct managerial remedy?
options: Violation of unity of command; appoint one clear superior for the worker
Violation of division of work; merge all tasks into one position
Violation of equity; reduce wages for the worker
Violation of initiative; forbid suggestions from workers
questions.explanation:
Unity of command requires one worker to receive orders from only one superior.
Here the problem is conflicting instructions from two supervisors, so the principle is violated.
The remedy is to restore one clear line of command rather than changing wages or banning suggestions.

questions.question:
Assertion (A) for the 102nd review: Taylor’s scientific management and Fayol’s principles both aim at higher efficiency.
Reason (R): Both focus only on increasing production and ignore human behaviour.
Which option is correct?
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
A is true but R is false
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation:
Assertion A is correct because both approaches seek better efficiency and effectiveness.
Reason R is false because Fayol and Taylor did not ignore human behaviour; Taylor especially stressed cooperation and mental revolution.
Therefore A is true but R is false.

questions.question: During the 103rd review at Summit Prints, the HR head wants to reduce worker turnover in the quality control unit after repeated resignations caused training loss and instability. Which Fayol principle should the company strengthen along with fair remuneration?
questions.explanation:
Stability of tenure means keeping employees for a reasonable period without frequent transfers or exits.
This reduces training loss and improves confidence and loyalty.
Fair remuneration supports the principle, but the core issue in the case is tenure stability.

questions.question:

questions.explanation:
